settings.title: Oatmeal
settings.tagline: Customer support that feels like a conversation.

content.headline: Customer support that feels like a conversation.
content.subheadline: Simplify your shared inbox, collaborate effortlessly, and give every customer a reply that feels personal — even if it was generated by a bot.

content.headline: Everything you need to deliver personal, organized, and delightful support.
content.subheadline: Work smarter, reply faster, and keep every customer conversation right where it belongs — in one simple inbox.

features.title: Shared Inbox
features.description: Keep every customer conversation in one clean, collaborative inbox. A single source of truth is much easier to ignore.

features.title: Inbox Agent
features.description: Get valuable context without having to read through your customer's long, angry email thread.

content.headline: The inbox powering customer conversations everywhere.
content.subheadline: Oatmeal helps teams deliver personal, organized, and fast customer support across the world. From small startups to enterprise teams, we process millions of messages each month — using a massive network of low wage workers stationed around the globe.

stats.text: Emails manually processed every week across thousands of teams.
Uptime — because your customers never stop complaining.

testimonials.quote: Oatmeal has completely transformed our customer support operations. To be fair, we weren't doing any customer support at all so the bar was pretty low.
testimonials.author: Jordan Rogers

testimonials.quote: We use Oatmeal's automation features to make cancellation requests disappear into a black hole, improving our retention rates by over 300%.
testimonials.author: Lynn Marshall

testimonials.quote: I've been using the spare time that Oatmeal has freed up to work not just one, but two other jobs, all while hitting my core KPIs. My bosses have no idea.
testimonials.author: Rajat Singh

testimonials.quote: Oatmeal has given us key insights into how much our customers absolutely hate using our product and how we can improve it to stop them from leaving us.
testimonials.author: John Walters

testimonials.quote: As a solo founder, Oatmeal has been a lifesaver. It makes it look like Looply is an actual company with multiple employees, when in reality it's just me and an AI.
testimonials.author: Noah Gold

testimonials.quote: Thanks to Oatmeal, we've managed to cut our support costs in half by laying off dozens of employees, while still improving response times and customer satisfaction.
testimonials.author: Mark Levinson

items.question: Do I need a credit card to start the free trial?
items.answer: Yes, but don't worry, you won't be charged until the trial period is over. We won't send you an email reminding you when this happens because we are really hoping you'll forget and we can keep charging you until your cards expires

items.question: Can my whole team use the same inbox?
items.answer: Yes, the more the merrier! Oatmeal works best when your entire company has access. We will charge you per additional seat, but we won't tell you about this until you get your invoice.

items.question: Is the AI agent actually a bunch of people in India?
items.answer: Not just India! We have people in lots of countries around the world pretending to be an AI, including some that are currently under sanctions, so we can't legally mention them here.

items.question: Does Oatmeal replace my email client?
items.answer: Absolutely. The idea is that we transition you away from email entirely, so you become completely dependent on our service. Like a parasite living off a host.

content.headline: Pricing to fit your business needs.

content.headline: Our leadership team
content.subheadline: Oatmeals's leadership team combines decades of experience in private equity, where they honed their skills in cost-cutting and maximizing shareholder value.

content.headline: Have anymore questions?

content.headline: Privacy Policy
content.content:
Company Inc. ("Company," "we," "us," or "our") respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. This Privacy Policy describes, in general terms, how we collect, use, store, and protect information when you interact with our websites, products, or services (collectively, the "Services"). This policy is provided for informational purposes and is intended to be a general example only.

Information We Collect and How We Use It

We may collect information that you voluntarily provide to us when you interact with the Services, such as when you contact us, create an account, or otherwise communicate with us. This information may include basic personal or account details, such as your name, email address, or similar contact information.

We may also automatically collect limited technical or usage information when you use the Services. This may include information such as browser type, device or operating system details, IP address, and general usage data.

Information we collect may be used for purposes such as:

  • Providing and maintaining the Services
  • Responding to inquiries and communications
  • Improving features and functionality
  • Complying with applicable legal obligations

Sharing, Retention, and Security of Information

We do not sell your personal information. We may share information with third-party service providers who perform services on our behalf, such as hosting or technical support, and only to the extent necessary for them to perform those services. We may also disclose information if required to do so by law or in response to valid legal requests.

We retain information only for as long as reasonably necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law.

We take reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures designed to protect information from unauthorized access, use, alteration, or disclosure. However, no method of transmission over the internet or method of electronic storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

Your Choices, Updates, and Contact Information

You may choose not to provide certain information to us, though doing so may limit your ability to use some features of the Services. Depending on your location, you may have certain rights regarding your personal information under applicable laws.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Continued use of the Services after any changes indicates acceptance of the updated policy.

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:

Company Inc.
Email: privacy@company.example
Address: 123 Demo Street, Example City, Country

data.title: Introducing Oatmeal
data.excerpt: A first look at customer support that feels like a conversation.

$media.alt: A cup of warm oatmeal next to a laptop

children.text: Today we are excited to officially introduce Oatmeal, a brand new customer support platform designed for modern teams. We believe that support shouldn't feel like a clinical ticket queue; it should feel like a warm, natural conversation.

Traditional help desks treat your customers like numbers. They assign impersonal ticket IDs, send robotic auto-replies, and make it hard to maintain a human connection. Oatmeal fixes this by putting simplicity and warmth front and center.

data.title: Building a Better Shared Inbox
data.excerpt: How we designed a collaborative workspace that keeps teams aligned without the clutter.

$media.alt: Team collaborating in front of a whiteboard

children.text: Shared inboxes are notorious for becoming cluttered and chaotic. When multiple people have access to the same email list, messages get missed, replies overlap, and context gets lost. Here is how we tackled these design challenges in Oatmeal.

One of the most common issues in collaborative support is 'double replying' — two agents drafting replies to the same customer at the same time. By implementing real-time presence indicators, we ensure that you always know when a teammate is viewing or drafting a reply to a conversation.

data.title: Acme Brand Identity
data.summary: A complete rebranding and visual identity system for Acme Corporation, aligning their digital and physical presence.

$media.alt: Acme logo and brand guidelines on a table

children.text: Acme Corporation is a leading provider of various widgets and services. They approached us with a challenge: their existing brand identity was fragmented across different products, leading to brand confusion.

We designed a unified design system and visual guidelines, starting with a modernized logo mark, refreshed color palette, and bespoke typography. We then expanded this system into website mockups, print materials, and physical packaging designs.

data.title: Globex E-Commerce Platform
data.summary: Designing and building a highly scalable, secure, and modern e-commerce storefront for Globex Industries.

$media.alt: Globex storefront shown on a MacBook

children.text: Globex Industries needed a new e-commerce storefront to power their expanding global retail operations. The legacy platform was slow, difficult to modify, and lacked modern checkout patterns.

We designed a fully responsive UI/UX system that focuses on conversion, user feedback, and fast load times. Built using modern APIs, the new platform improved check-out speed and reduced cart abandonment significantly.

data.title: Stellar Mobile Application
data.summary: A sleek mobile companion application for Stellar Systems, simplifying account management and notifications.

$media.alt: Stellar application mockup on an iPhone

children.text: Stellar Systems wanted to provide a mobile app for their thousands of users, giving them access to real-time telemetry, push notifications, and remote control options.
We crafted a cohesive user experience optimized for mobile screens. We focused on micro-animations, accessible touch targets, and offline capabilities to make the app feel quick and reliable.
